stations.name: Norman Park railway station
stations.walk_minutes_note: Within the suburb. Most streets are within a 5 to 12 minute walk.

stations.name: Coorparoo railway station
stations.walk_minutes_note: Closer for some southern Norman Park addresses.

trains.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 10 to 14 minutes to Central station off-peak.

buses.note: Bus routes serve Norman Park via Wynnum Road and Bennetts Road.

driving.motorway_access: Story Bridge for the CBD. M3 corridor via Coorparoo for southern travel.
driving.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 8 to 12 minutes to Brisbane CBD off-peak.

connections: Norman Creek bikeway running through the suburb
Walking access to the Bulimba and Hawthorne strips from northern streets

parks: Mowbray Park accessible via Riding Road
lifestyle.shops_dining_note: Norman Park itself has limited dining within the suburb. The Bulimba Oxford Street strip and the Hawthorne Garage precinct are within five to ten minutes by car. Coorparoo Square sits south for additional retail and cinema.
lifestyle.topography_note: Norman Park has a clear ridge running through it. Ridge streets command a premium and the topography matters in the pricing conversation.

pockets.name: Ridge streets
pockets.character: Elevated blocks with city or district outlooks. Premium pocket. Character queenslanders and architect-designed builds.

pockets.name: Station-adjacent
pockets.character: Walking distance to Norman Park station. Transport-focused pocket with mix of character and post-war stock.

pockets.name: Norman Creek edge
pockets.character: Streets bordering the creek parkland. Privacy and green-space access from the doorstep.

buyer_profile.note: Parents targeting the Norman Park catchment and the family-home stock on the ridge streets.
Buyers moving up from Coorparoo or East Brisbane for the topography, character, and train access combination.
Buyers priced out of Bulimba and Hawthorne who want the inner-east lifestyle at a different entry point.
